← 返回文章列表
The Atlantic

A New Phase of the AI-Jobs Panic

As politicians and tech executives propose ambitious plans to prepare for AI-driven job losses, many of those efforts may serve Silicon Valley's interests more than workers' needs.

✍️ The Atlantic 📅 原文：2026-07-10 / 精读：2026-07-17 📖 约 1,460 词 🎯 考研英语一难度

📋 文章大意

本文选自《大西洋月刊》，聚焦人工智能对就业市场的冲击以及美国各界的应对。作者通过自身参加政府人工智能素养课程的经历，指出当前官方准备措施过于表面；随后分析桑德斯参议员提出的“AI主权财富基金”、OpenAI对公共所有制的支持，以及科技巨头出资成立的政策实验机构 Raise Us 等方案。文章最终揭示：硅谷一方面推动自动化，另一方面又借“拯救就业”之名加深社会对 AI 工具的依赖，真正的劳动者保护可能被架空。

📰 正文精读

The U.S. government's AI literacy

/ˈlɪtərəsi/
n. 读写能力；素养
某领域的知识或能力 programs, like a text-based course from the Labor Department, offer trivial
/ˈtrɪviəl/
adj. 琐碎的；不重要的
微不足道、缺乏 seriousness 的事物 advice such as asking AI for side hustle ideas, proving insufficient for looming
/ˈluːmɪŋ/
adj. 逼近的；隐约出现的
似乎即将发生并令人担忧的 job losses. Politicians and tech executives have proposed more serious measures, including Senator Bernie Sanders' AI Sovereign Wealth Fund Act, which would give the government a 50% stake in AI companies, and OpenAI's support for public ownership. However, these efforts often serve Silicon Valley's interests. For instance, Anthropic's $350 million commitment includes a fellowship to integrate its Claude AI into nonprofits, essentially training workers to use AI rather than protecting them from displacement
/dɪsˈpleɪsmənt/
n. 替代；流离失所
被迫离开原有位置或工作. Raise Us, a new nonprofit led by former Biden officials, has raised over $500 million from tech companies to test policies like wage insurance, but its partnerships with AI firms raise concerns about conflicts of interest. Economists remain uncertain about the scale of disruption, but the tech industry's involvement in crafting solutions may prioritize promoting its own tools over genuine worker protection.

In late March, I started receiving daily texts from the federal government about AI.

"AI is changing how we work and live," one message read.

"You might feel curious, skeptical, or unsure—that's normal."

I had enrolled in an AI-literacy course from the Labor Department created to help workers succeed in the ChatGPT economy.

The weeklong program, created in partnership with an AI start-up and delivered by text message, was supposed to equip

/ɪˈkwɪp/
v. 使具备；装备
提供必要的知识、技能或工具 Americans with "foundational AI skills," according to an agency press release.

But the government's texts were not reassuring.

One message encouraged me to ask a bot for "side hustle ideas."

Another suggested that I brush up on my AI skills by doodling: "Grab a friend and see whose drawing of a hippo AI can recognize … and whose it mistakes for a lumpy potato."

When it comes to AI's threat to jobs, America has acted like a deer in the headlights.

For all the catastrophic

/ˌkætəˈstrɒfɪk/
adj. 灾难性的；极坏的
造成巨大破坏或失败的 messaging about looming
/ˈluːmɪŋ/
adj. 逼近的
即将来临并令人担忧的 layoffs, efforts to prepare for a major transition have lagged
/læɡd/
v. 落后；滞后
未能跟上应有的速度或进度.

After speaking with a roster

/ˈrɒstə(r)/
n. 名册；名单
一组人或项目的列表 of the nation's top business and political leaders, my colleague Josh Tyrangiel concluded earlier this year that no one seemed to have a plan for how to proceed.

Government efforts to shore up

/ʃɔːr ʌp/
phr. 支撑；巩固
通过援助或支持使某物更稳固 the labor market have been largely underwhelming
/ˌʌndəˈwelmɪŋ/
adj. 令人失望的；平庸的
未能给人留下深刻印象.

AI-literacy courses like the one I took are hardly a sufficient

/səˈfɪʃnt/
adj. 足够的；充分的
满足需求或达到目的 response if you believe that mass job loss is forthcoming; the Trump administration announced a goal last year to expand apprenticeship programs, but progress on that front has been middling
/ˈmɪdlɪŋ/
adj. 中等的；一般的
不好不坏的，平庸的.

Meanwhile, the tech industry has barreled ahead

/ˈbærəld əˈhed/
phr. 迅猛推进
快速而有力地向前发展
in its efforts to develop more capable models.

Earlier this year, Anthropic's Dario Amodei warned that "AI isn't a substitute

/ˈsʌbstɪtjuːt/
n. 替代品
可用来替代另一物的人或事物 for specific human jobs but rather a general labor substitute for humans."

But over the past several weeks, politicians and tech executives have started to make a show of

/meɪk ə ʃəʊ ɒv/
phr. 装出……的样子
故意表现出某种态度或行为
more seriously preparing the country for a future of AI layoffs.

As promising as these new efforts are, many of them also end up serving the interests of Silicon Valley.

Consider a flashy

/ˈflæʃi/
adj. 花哨的；引人注目的
外表华丽但可能缺乏实质的 proposal for state ownership of AI companies.

Last month, Senator Bernie Sanders proposed the AI Sovereign Wealth Fund Act, which would give the federal government a 50 percent stake in major AI companies including Anthropic and OpenAI.

The act would also give an appointed commission voting shares to help "stop bad decisions that will reap

/riːp/
v. 收获；招致
获得某种结果，常指负面的后果 massive job loss," Sanders said on a press call.

The legislation

/ˌledʒɪsˈleɪʃn/
n. 立法；法规
由立法机关通过的法律或法案, which calls for the tech industry to hand over trillions of dollars worth of stock, is unsurprising coming from Sanders, who regularly takes aim at
/teɪks eɪm æt/
phr. 抨击；把……作为目标
公开批评或攻击某人/某物 "Big Tech oligarchs."

What's surprising is the fact that at least one of the alleged Big Tech oligarchs

/ˈɒlɪɡɑːks/
n. 寡头
掌握巨大权力或财富的少数人 is on board with a watered-down
/ˈwɔːtəd daʊn/
adj. 淡化的；削弱强度的
降低原有力度或效果的 version of Sanders's proposal.

After Sanders first announced his plan, Sam Altman requested to meet with the senator.

The OpenAI CEO reportedly told Sanders that although he does not endorse

/ɪnˈdɔːs/
v. 赞同；支持
公开认可或支持 the exact plan, he supports the idea of public ownership of AI companies.

Altman has been flirting with

/ˈflɜːtɪŋ wɪð/
phr. 对……跃跃欲试
考虑或尝试但尚未认真投入 such ideas since even before ChatGPT was launched, and in April, OpenAI endorsed the creation of a "public wealth fund" that would give every citizen "a stake in AI-driven economic growth."

Still, it's difficult to imagine a future in which our dysfunctional

/dɪsˈfʌŋkʃənl/
adj. 功能失调的
无法正常运作的 Congress passes a law to take a stake in AI companies, and then the government seamlessly
/ˈsiːmləsli/
adv. 无缝地；顺利地
平滑、无间断地 redistributes the earnings to the rest of us.

And regardless, such arrangements are unlikely to save a flailing

/ˈfleɪlɪŋ/
adj. 挣扎的；失控的
慌乱而无明确方向的 labor market.

Sanders's aggressive wealth fund, for example, aims to start by paying roughly $1,000 to each American annually—which is not trivial, but is also far from a solution to mass unemployment.

And private companies don't give away equity out of altruism

/ˈæltruɪzəm/
n. 利他主义
无私关心他人福祉.

If the government were to seize

/siːz/
v. 夺取；控制
用强力取得或占有 control of AI companies, the country could end up responsible for bailouts
/ˈbeɪlaʊts/
n. 紧急救助
政府或机构对困境企业的资金援助, and public ownership could dilute
/daɪˈluːt/
v. 稀释；削弱
降低强度、纯度或影响力 the demand for more onerous
/ˈəʊnərəs/
adj. 繁重的；费力的
承担沉重负担的 regulation.

Anthropic has also said that it would like to invest in researching "AI sovereign wealth funds" as a possible response to extreme unemployment.

In May, OpenAI's nonprofit foundation announced that it would spend $250 million on grants and partnerships to support the forthcoming economic transition, while also building its own team to seed new ideas.

And last month, Anthropic committed

/kəˈmɪtɪd/
v. 承诺；投入
保证投入资源或履行义务 $350 million toward similar efforts, much of which will fund "major research trials and program evaluation on promising public policies."

Economists, for their part, remain unsure about what types of policy interventions

/ˌɪntəˈvenʃnz/
n. 干预措施
为改变局面而采取的行动 could best mitigate
/ˈmɪtɪɡeɪt/
v. 减轻；缓和
降低负面影响的严重程度 future problems, or really how big those problems are likely to be.

AI has not yet led to widespread job losses, and many economists aren't convinced that a jobs apocalypse

/əˈpɒkəlɪps/
n. 大灾难；末日
毁灭性的大规模事件 is inevitable.

But even absent the extreme, if moderate labor displacement occurs quickly, America could be in for a real shock.

In that light, one new nonprofit, Raise Us, launched last month with the intention of testing out different policies to see what works.

Led by Gina Raimondo, Joe Biden's commerce secretary, and Eric Holcomb, a former Republican governor of Indiana, the organization has already raised more than $500 million from private companies and philanthropies

/fɪˈlænθrəpiz/
n. 慈善机构
致力于公益捐助的组织.

Some of that funding comes straight from Silicon Valley: Anthropic, Amazon, Microsoft, and the OpenAI Foundation have contributed cash to the effort and are among the organization's founding partners.

Raimondo told me that she hopes that by doing smaller-scale experiments now at the state level, the organization will gather data on which interventions are most effective, helping inform

/ɪnˈfɔːm/
v. 影响；为……提供依据
为决策或行动提供知识基础 federal policy efforts down the line.

For example, the group is interested in testing wage insurance.

Under such a policy, if someone loses a job and accepts work at lower pay, wage insurance would aim to temporarily fill some of the gap.

There is "some positive evidence" in favor of the policy, David Autor, an MIT economist who serves as an unpaid adviser to Raise Us, told me, but it has "never been done at any significant scale in the United States."

The group also plans to invest in a slew

/sluː/
n. 大量
许多，大量 of other interventions, including apprenticeship programs and career-navigation tools.

Overall, the group's efforts will likely be useful in generating real-world policy evidence that is "undersupplied" and "extremely valuable," Susan Athey, a Stanford economist who is not affiliated with

/əˈfɪlieɪtɪd wɪð/
phr. 隶属于；与……有关联
与某组织存在正式关联 Raise Us, told me.

At the same time, the tech industry's association with Raise Us is also a play to boost its flagging

/ˈflæɡɪŋ/
adj. 衰退的；疲软的
逐渐失去活力或兴趣的 reputation.

Brad Smith, the vice chair and president of Microsoft, told me that the Raise Us coalition is similar to the public-private partnerships that formed in response to the pandemic.

It's a compelling

/kəmˈpelɪŋ/
adj. 有说服力的；引人注目的
令人信服或难以忽视的 comparison, but a flawed
/flɔːd/
adj. 有缺陷的
存在错误或不足的 one: Imagine if the vaccine companies involved with Operation Warp Speed were also engineering more sophisticated viruses.

Silicon Valley has wedged

/wedʒd/
v. 楔入；使陷入
强行挤入或使处于困境 itself into a strange position.

AI companies are racing to automate work while simultaneously preparing to parachute in as saviors

/ˈpærəʃuːt ɪn æz ˈseɪvjəz/
phr. 以救世主姿态空降
突然出现并声称解决问题.

Of course, shepherding a smooth labor transition is in Silicon Valley's self-interest.

An AI backlash is brewing, and tech companies' reputations will only further degrade

/dɪˈɡreɪd/
v. 降级；恶化
降低质量、地位或价值 if layoffs mount.

But all this hullabaloo

/ˌhʌləbəˈluː/
n. 喧嚣；大吵大闹
嘈杂的争论或活动 around wealth funds and public-private partnerships is about much more than just optics
/ˈɒptɪks/
n. 公众形象；表象
某事在外人看起来的样子.

AI companies are leveraging

/ˈlevərɪdʒɪŋ/
v. 利用；杠杆化
利用某种局势以获得优势 the current anxiety over layoffs to induce
/ɪnˈdjuːs/
v. 诱导；引起
促使某人做某事或导致某结果 greater dependence on their bots.

Several industry-supported efforts to ward off

/wɔːd ɒf/
phr. 防止；避开
采取行动避免危险或问题 a jobs crisis involve training people to better use AI.

In Maryland, for example, Raise Us is supporting the launch of a start-up accelerator

/ˈstɑːt ʌp ækˈseləreɪtə(r)/
phr. 创业加速器
帮助初创企业快速成长的项目或机构, which will provide displaced workers with financial assistance—and access to AI tools to help them start businesses.

And almost half of Anthropic's $350 million commitment will go toward creating a national fellowship called Claude Corps.

Through the program, Anthropic will pay early-career workers $85,000 each to spend a year working to integrate Claude across at least 400 nonprofits.

The solution to Claude's threatening entry-level workers' jobs: Hire young people to further evangelize

/ɪˈvændʒəlaɪz/
v. 鼓吹；宣传
热情传播某种理念或产品 Claude.

📝 考研核心词汇表

单词 音标 词性 中文释义 例句
literacy/ˈlɪtərəsi/n.读写能力；素养Financial literacy is essential for managing personal wealth.
trivial/ˈtrɪviəl/adj.琐碎的；不重要的Don't waste time on trivial details.
looming/ˈluːmɪŋ/adj.逼近的；隐约出现的The looming deadline increased everyone's stress.
displacement/dɪsˈpleɪsmənt/n.替代；流离失所Automation may cause the displacement of many workers.
equip/ɪˈkwɪp/v.使具备；装备The course equips students with practical skills.
catastrophic/ˌkætəˈstrɒfɪk/adj.灾难性的The flood had catastrophic effects on the region.
lag/læɡ/v.落后；滞后Progress on the project continues to lag.
roster/ˈrɒstə(r)/n.名册；名单The roster of speakers includes several experts.
shore up/ʃɔːr ʌp/phr.支撑；巩固The government took measures to shore up the economy.
underwhelming/ˌʌndəˈwelmɪŋ/adj.令人失望的；平庸的The product launch was underwhelming.
sufficient/səˈfɪʃnt/adj.足够的；充分的Is there sufficient evidence to support the claim?
middling/ˈmɪdlɪŋ/adj.中等的；一般的The restaurant received middling reviews.
barrel ahead/ˈbærəld əˈhed/phr.迅猛推进The company barreled ahead with its expansion plan.
substitute/ˈsʌbstɪtjuːt/n.替代品There is no substitute for hard work.
flashy/ˈflæʃi/adj.花哨的；引人注目的He avoided flashy displays of wealth.
legislation/ˌledʒɪsˈleɪʃn/n.立法；法规New legislation was introduced to protect consumers.
oligarch/ˈɒlɪɡɑːk/n.寡头The country is controlled by a small group of oligarchs.
watered-down/ˈwɔːtəd daʊn/adj.淡化的；削弱强度的The final proposal was a watered-down version.
endorse/ɪnˈdɔːs/v.赞同；支持The committee refused to endorse the plan.
dysfunctional/dɪsˈfʌŋkʃənl/adj.功能失调的The dysfunctional team failed to meet its goals.
seamlessly/ˈsiːmləsli/adv.无缝地；顺利地The two systems work together seamlessly.
flailing/ˈfleɪlɪŋ/adj.挣扎的；失控的The flailing company sought emergency funding.
altruism/ˈæltruɪzəm/n.利他主义His decision was motivated by altruism, not profit.
seize/siːz/v.夺取；控制The army attempted to seize power.
bailout/ˈbeɪlaʊt/n.紧急救助The bank received a massive government bailout.
dilute/daɪˈluːt/v.稀释；削弱The reform was diluted by political compromise.
onerous/ˈəʊnərəs/adj.繁重的；费力的The contract imposed onerous conditions.
commit/kəˈmɪt/v.承诺；投入The company committed $10 million to research.
intervention/ˌɪntəˈvenʃn/n.干预措施Government intervention stabilized the market.
mitigate/ˈmɪtɪɡeɪt/v.减轻；缓和We must take steps to mitigate climate change.
apocalypse/əˈpɒkəlɪps/n.大灾难；末日The film depicts a post-apocalypse world.

🔗 短语搭配

side hustle
副业；兼职
原文：One message encouraged me to ask a bot for "side hustle ideas."
a deer in the headlights
（因惊恐或困惑而）呆若木鸡
原文：When it comes to AI's threat to jobs, America has acted like a deer in the headlights.
shore up
支撑；巩固
原文：Government efforts to shore up the labor market have been largely underwhelming.
barrel ahead
迅猛推进
原文：Meanwhile, the tech industry has barreled ahead in its efforts to develop more capable models.
take aim at
抨击；把……作为目标
原文：Sanders, who regularly takes aim at "Big Tech oligarchs."
out of altruism
出于利他主义
原文：And private companies don't give away equity out of altruism.
affiliated with
隶属于；与……有关联
原文：Susan Athey, a Stanford economist who is not affiliated with Raise Us, told me.
ward off
防止；避开
原文：Several industry-supported efforts to ward off a jobs crisis involve training people to better use AI.

🔍 长难句解析

AI-literacy courses like the one I took are hardly a sufficient response if you believe that mass job loss is forthcoming; the Trump administration announced a goal last year to expand apprenticeship programs, but progress on that front has been middling.
【结构分析】本句由分号连接两个并列分句。前一分句中，"like the one I took"为介词短语修饰"courses"；"if you believe..."为条件状语从句，其中"that mass job loss is forthcoming"为宾语从句。后一分句中，"but"连接转折关系，"on that front"为固定搭配，表示“在那一方面”。【句意】如果你相信大规模失业即将来临，那么像我参加的那种人工智能素养课程很难说是充分的应对措施；特朗普政府去年宣布了扩大学徒计划的目标，但在这方面的进展平平。
If the government were to seize control of AI companies, the country could end up responsible for bailouts, and public ownership could dilute the demand for more onerous regulation.
【结构分析】"If the government were to seize..."为虚拟条件句，表示与现在事实相反的假设；主句由"and"连接两个并列分句，分别说明两种可能后果。"end up responsible for"表示“最终不得不承担……责任”。【句意】如果政府要接管人工智能公司，国家最终可能要为紧急救助负责，而且公有制可能会削弱对更繁重监管的需求。
Raimondo told me that she hopes that by doing smaller-scale experiments now at the state level, the organization will gather data on which interventions are most effective, helping inform federal policy efforts down the line.
【结构分析】主句为"Raimondo told me that..."，that 引导宾语从句；宾语从句中又嵌套"that she hopes that..."结构；"by doing smaller-scale experiments..."为介词短语作方式状语；"which interventions are most effective"为介词 on 的宾语从句；"helping inform..."为现在分词短语作结果状语。【句意】雷蒙多告诉我，她希望通过现在在州一级开展小规模实验，该组织能够收集哪些干预措施最有效的数据，从而为后续的联邦政策工作提供依据。

✅ 阅读理解练习

1. 【主旨大意题】What is the main idea of the passage?
  1. AI will inevitably cause mass unemployment in the near future.
  2. The U.S. government has effectively prepared workers for the AI transition through literacy courses.
  3. Politicians and tech firms are proposing responses to AI job losses, but many serve Silicon Valley's interests rather than workers.
  4. Senator Sanders's AI Sovereign Wealth Fund Act will solve America's unemployment problem.
正确答案：C
解析：文章首先批评政府 AI 素养课程流于表面，接着分析桑德斯的财富基金提案、OpenAI 的公有制表态以及 Raise Us 等科技资助项目，最终指出这些举措往往更多服务于硅谷利益而非真正保护劳动者。A 项“必然导致大规模失业”过于绝对；B 项与文中“underwhelming”“insufficient”相反；D 项与文中“far from a solution”相悖。
2. 【细节理解题】According to the author, what is the real purpose behind some AI companies' support for worker-protection initiatives?
  1. To demonstrate their social responsibility and gain public trust.
  2. To deepen public dependence on their AI products.
  3. To comply with new federal regulations on AI ownership.
  4. To reduce their investment in research and development.
正确答案：B
解析：文章末段明确指出"AI companies are leveraging the current anxiety over layoffs to induce greater dependence on their bots"，并举了 Claude Corps 培训年轻人推广 Claude 的例子。A 项只是表象；C、D 两项文中未提及。
3. 【推理判断题】What can be inferred from Brad Smith's comparison between Raise Us and pandemic-era public-private partnerships?
  1. The author believes the comparison perfectly captures the nature of Raise Us.
  2. The author thinks the comparison is inspiring and should be promoted.
  3. The author considers the comparison flawed because AI companies may benefit from the very problem they claim to solve.
  4. The author agrees that vaccine companies should also develop more sophisticated viruses.
正确答案：C
解析：作者先承认该比较“compelling”，随即用"but a flawed one"转折，并类比“如果参与曲速行动的疫苗公司同时也在研发更复杂的病毒”，暗示 AI 公司一边推动自动化一边充当拯救者，可能从问题本身获利。A、B 与"flawed"矛盾；D 项为字面曲解。

💡 阅读技巧提示

  • 把握“现象—评价—例证”结构：本文先描述政府 AI 课程的表面化，再评价各类应对方案，最后用具体公司项目作例证。理清这一结构有助于快速定位作者态度。
  • 识别讽刺与转折：文中多次出现 but、however、yet 等转折词，以及"deer in the headlights""parachute in as saviors"等隐喻，往往是作者真实立场的信号。
  • 警惕“观点—反讽”选项：考研阅读常把作者批评的观点或他人的比较设成干扰项，要注意区分作者态度与文中人物态度。
  • 积累政经类话题词汇：legislation, intervention, mitigate, displacement, bailout, oligarch 等为考研英语一阅读高频词，建议结合文章语境记忆。
  • 注意抽象名词的指代：文中 optics、altruism、displacement、evangelize 等词在特定语境下含义较抽象，做题时需回归上下文判断。

考研英语外刊精读 · 每日更新 · 来源：The Atlantic

原文链接：https://www.theatlantic.com/technology/archive/2026/07/ai-jobs-panic-silicon-valley/683514/
